text: BUSINESS REGISTRATION IN UGANDA — WHEN AND HOW. Not every micro-enterprise needs formal registration immediately. Sole traders earning below UGX 50 million/year can operate with just a trading license. However, formal registration helps with: opening business bank accounts, getting supplier credit, accessing government tenders, and building credibility. Types: (1) Sole Proprietorship — UGX 25,000 at URSB, simplest form. (2) Partnership — UGX 100,000 at URSB, for 2+ people sharing business. (3) Private Limited Company — UGX 350,000 at URSB, separate legal entity, best for growth. Start as sole proprietor, upgrade as you grow.

TRADING LICENSE — REQUIRED FOR ALL BUSINESSES. In Kampala: Apply at your Division Office (Nakawa, Makindye, Rubaga, Kawempe, or Central). Cost: UGX 50,000-200,000/year depending on business size and category. Outside Kampala: Apply at your Sub-County or Town Council office. Cost is usually lower (UGX 30,000-100,000/year). Documents needed: National ID, passport photos, proof of business premises (tenancy agreement or ownership), completed application form. Processing time: 1-5 working days. Penalty for operating without: Fine up to UGX 500,000 or closure. Renewal: Annual, usually January-March. TIP: Apply early in the year to avoid long queues.

URA TAX OBLIGATIONS FOR SMALL BUSINESSES. Every business must register for a Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) — it's FREE. Register at ura.go.ug or any URA service centre. Tax categories: (1) Presumptive Tax — for businesses with turnover UGX 10M-150M/year: pay 1.5% of annual turnover. Filed annually. Very simple. (2) Income Tax — for turnover above UGX 150M: standard rates apply (30% corporate, 10-40% individual progressive). (3) VAT Registration — MANDATORY when turnover exceeds UGX 150M/year. Before that, optional. Filing deadlines: Annual return by June 30 each year for individuals. Penalty for non-compliance: 5% of tax due per month. Most micro-enterprises fall under Presumptive Tax — very affordable.

NSSF (NATIONAL SOCIAL SECURITY FUND) OBLIGATIONS. If you have 5 or more employees, NSSF registration is MANDATORY. Contribution: 15% of each employee's gross salary — employer pays 10%, employee pays 5%. Register at any NSSF branch. Monthly remittance deadline: 15th of following month. Penalty for late payment: 5% per month. Even if you have fewer than 5 employees, voluntary NSSF contributions are recommended — it's a forced savings mechanism. As a self-employed person, you can make voluntary contributions to build your retirement fund. Minimum voluntary contribution: UGX 5,000/month.

SECTOR-SPECIFIC LICENSES AND PERMITS. Food businesses: Food Handler's Certificate (UGX 50,000, medical exam + hygiene training, valid 1 year). Public Health Permit for premises (UGX 100,000-200,000/year). KCCA food inspection compliance. Poultry/Livestock: Livestock Movement Permit (UGX 20,000 per movement between districts). Annual livestock trader's license. Veterinary health certificate for meat sales. Mobile Money: Agent registration through authorized super-agent (free but requires National ID + premises). Salon/Barbershop: Health and Safety compliance certificate (UGX 50,000-100,000/year). Construction/Welding: Environmental compliance for metal workshops in residential areas. Transport (Boda-Boda): Valid driving permit Class A (UGX 100,000), annual vehicle inspection, third-party insurance (UGX 50,000-100,000/year), stage membership fees (UGX 5,000-20,000/month).
